results.question: compare Active user month over month
results.answerText:
Here's the MoM breakdown for {{field:user_active_monthly.mau}}:

| Month | MAU | MoM Δ |
|-------|-----|--------|
| 2026-03 | 2,437,744 | — |
| 2026-04 | 955,482 | **−60.8%** |
| 2026-05 | 754,639 | **−21.0%** |
| 2026-06 | 495,501 | **−34.3%** ⚠️ |

**Key takeaways:**
- May → Jun is down **−259,138 users (−34.3%)**, but June is only 22/30 days complete — the final number will be higher.
- The steepest drop was Mar → Apr (−60.8%), suggesting a large cohort that month didn't return.
- May to Jun trend is consistent with the prior months' decline; worth watching at month-end.

> **⚠️ June caveat:** {{field:user_active_monthly.log_year_month}} `= '2026-06'` captures users active so far this month. A fair MoM comparison can only be made once the month closes on Jun 30.

results.question: show ARPDAU last 7 days
results.answerText:
ARPDAU for cfm_vn — Jun 16–18 (latest data available):

| Date | Revenue (VND) | DAU | **ARPDAU (VND)** |
|---|---|---|---|
| Jun 16 | 222.0B | 82,112 | **2,704,120** |
| Jun 17 | 221.4B | 80,970 | **2,733,856** |
| Jun 18 | 86.5B | 20,619 | ⚠️ ~4.2M (partial) |

Jun 16–17 are solid: ARPDAU is stable around **≈2.72M VND/DAU**. Jun 18 shows a sharp DAU drop (~75% lower) with proportionally lower revenue — the pipeline hasn't finished loading that day yet, so the ratio is inflated and should be ignored.

The artifact uses {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} ÷ {{field:active_daily.dau}} per day. Click the card to open in Playground and adjust the date range.

results.question: ARPDAU this month
results.answerText:
ARPDAU for CFM VN in June 2026 is running in the **~2.55M – 2.69M VND** range across the first 10 days sampled. A few observations:

- {{field:active_daily.dau}} is drifting down (92K → 85K from Jun 1–10), while {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} stays relatively flat (~228B–238B VND/day).
- As a result, **ARPDAU is actually ticking up** — fewer active users but stable revenue means each active user monetizes more heavily.

The Playground artifact carries both raw measures so you can cross-reference revenue and DAU directly. Open it to see the full June 30-day series.

results.question: compare ARPDAU month over month
results.answerText:
Here's the MoM breakdown for cfm_vn:

| Period | Revenue (VND) | DAU-days | **ARPDAU** |
|--------|--------------|----------|------------|
| May 2026 | 344.5B | 207,593 | **1,659,673** |
| Jun 2026 MTD | 308.5B | 159,589 | **1,932,795 (+16.5%)** |

**The signal:** ARPDAU is up +16.5%, but both components are *declining* — revenue fell 10.4% while DAU fell 23.2%. ARPDAU rises precisely because {{field:active_daily.dau}} is shrinking faster than {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}}. This is a **DAU-drain pattern**, not a genuine monetisation improvement — the low/non-paying user base is eroding, leaving a higher-spending core behind.

> ⚠️ **June is MTD (22 of 30 days).** Revenue often concentrates in the first half of the month, so the gap may narrow as June closes. Re-run on Jul 1 for a clean full-month comparison.
results.artifactTitle: ARPDAU — This Month vs Last Month (MoM)

results.question: show ARPPU last 7 days
results.answerText:
ARPPU trended between **~560K – 642K VND** over the 3 days with data. Jun 18 shows fewer paying users (186 vs ~1,200 on prior days) which likely reflects an incomplete data pull for that date rather than a real drop — the pipeline lags behind Jun 22 by ~4 days.

The artifact pulls {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} and {{field:user_recharge_daily.paying_users}} daily; ARPPU is computed as their ratio. Open in Playground to drill by {{field:user_recharge_daily.country_code}}, {{field:user_recharge_daily.vip_level}}, or {{field:user_recharge_daily.payment_channel}}.

results.question: ARPPU this month
results.answerText:
ARPPU trended from ~₫577k on Jun 1 up to a peak of **₫845k on Jun 12** (likely an event/recharge campaign — paying-user count also spiked to 3,502 that day), then settled in the ₫630–660k range Jun 13–15. The early-week dip on Jun 5–6 shows the inverse: a mass-participation event pulled in many lower-value payers (~3,979 on Jun 5), dragging ARPPU down to ₫439k despite high total revenue.

ARPPU = {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} ÷ {{field:user_recharge_daily.paying_users}}, computed per day. Data covers Jun 1–15 (month in progress).

results.question: ARPU this month
results.answerText:
**~49,978 VND** is the current {{field:mf_users.arpu_vnd}} snapshot.

One caveat: {{field:mf_users.arpu_vnd}} is a **lifetime metric** (total LTV ÷ total registered users) materialized as a snapshot — the underlying `mf_users` rollup is keyed on `install_date`, not a daily time-series. There is no monthly-grain ARPU pre-aggregation for cfm_vn, so isolating "June only" revenue ÷ "June only" active users isn't possible from this cube directly.

If you need **revenue this month** as a proxy, I can query {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd}} for June 2026 instead.

results.question: show Churner last 7 days
results.answerText:
The artifact shows D30 churners — users from each install cohort who did **not** return on their Day 30 — for the last 7 days window.

**What the data says:**

| Install Date | Churned D30 | Cohort Size | Churn Rate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Jun 16 | 4,846 | 4,846 | 100% |
| Jun 17 | 4,755 | 4,755 | 100% |
| Jun 18 | 290 | 290 | 100% |
| Jun 19–22 | — | — | No data yet |

⚠️ **Two things to flag:**

1. **100% D30 churn rate across all 3 cohorts** is anomalous — it means {{field:retention.retained_d30}} = 0 for every cohort in this window. This may indicate a data pipeline issue or an actual retention crisis worth investigating.
2. **Jun 19–22 have no data** — D30 churn requires 30 days of observation after install, so cohorts installed after Jun 18 won't have D30 measurements until mid-July.

If you're looking for **recently lapsed users** (users who were active and went quiet in the last 7 days), that would be a different metric not captured by this D30 cohort model. Let me know if you'd like to explore that angle instead.
results.artifactTitle: D30 Churners by Install Cohort — Last 7 Days

results.question: Churner this month
results.answerText:
**149,196 D30 churners** from the May 2026 install cohort — these are users whose 30-day milestone falls in June, making them the cohort most relevant to "churn this month."

A few things to note:

- **Cohort size**: 154,060 users installed in May 2026
- **Retained at D30**: 4,864 (~3.2%)
- **D30 churn rate**: ~96.8% — high but within the normal mobile game range (D30 retention of 3–5% is typical for the genre)
- The **June 2026 cohort** can't be evaluated yet — today is Jun 22, so D30 data for June installs won't be available until late July. That's why the May cohort is the right anchor for "churner this month."

The artifact uses {{field:retention.churned_d30}} and {{field:retention.cohort_size}} from the `retention` cube, keyed by {{field:retention.install_date}}.

results.question: compare Churner month over month
results.answerText:
**Churner MoM — Apr vs May 2026** ✅

| | Apr 2026 | May 2026 | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| {{field:retention.churned_d30}} | 266,918 | 149,196 | **–117,722 (–44.1%)** |
| {{field:retention.cohort_size}} | 276,262 | 154,060 | –122,202 (–44.2%) |
| Churn rate | 96.62% | 96.84% | **+0.22 pp** |

**Verdict:** The raw churner count fell sharply in May, but that's entirely a volume effect — fewer installs → fewer churners. The D30 churn **rate** barely moved (+0.22 pp). Retention quality is flat, not improving.

> **Note:** June 2026 cohort excluded — D30 hasn't fully elapsed for June installs (today is Jun 22), so those numbers would be incomplete.

results.question: show Dormant user last 7 days
results.answerText:
**~6.4M users** have been inactive for 7+ days across 4 lifecycle stages:

| Lifecycle Stage | Count | Share |
|---|---|---|
| {{field:mf_users.lifecycle_stage}} = `churned` | 4.93M | 77% |
| {{field:mf_users.lifecycle_stage}} = `dormant` | 1.05M | 17% |
| {{field:mf_users.lifecycle_stage}} = `active_30d` | 353K | 6% |
| {{field:mf_users.lifecycle_stage}} = `active_7d` | 30K | <1% |

The query filters {{field:mf_users.days_since_last_active}} `≥ 7` on the User Master Profile — a point-in-time snapshot, not a rolling 7-day window. The small `active_7d` / `active_30d` rows are boundary users whose lifecycle stage was computed at a slightly earlier cutoff than today's `days_since_last_active`.

**Core dormant segment** (stage = `dormant`) is **1.05M users** — these are the re-engagement candidates. The `churned` group (4.93M) is likely beyond the standard win-back window.
results.artifactTitle: Dormant Users (Inactive ≥ 7 Days) by Lifecycle Stage

results.question: Dormant user this month
results.answerText:
**~386,734 dormant users this month** — active in May 2026 but zero logins in June so far.

This is powered by {{field:mf_users.lapsed_this_month_count}}, a pre-aggregated snapshot on the user master profile. The number reflects the current state as of today (June 22) and will shrink as some of these users return before month-end.

Want to dig deeper? A few natural next steps:

- Break down by {{field:mf_users.payer_tier}} to see how many dormant users are paying vs. non-paying
- Filter to {{field:mf_users.at_risk_paying}} segment (paying users gone inactive 7–30 days) for a winback priority list
- Compare dormant count month-over-month to see if the trend is worsening

results.question: show LTV last 7 days
results.answerText:
LTV dipped from **~153,395 VND** (Jun 16) to **~132,339 VND** (Jun 17) — a ~14% drop day-over-day. Data cuts off at Jun 17; Jun 18–22 rows are empty due to pipeline lag.

> ⚠️ **Semantic note:** This is the *daily cohort proxy* — {{field:game_key_metrics.rev}} ÷ {{field:game_key_metrics.nru}} on the same date, not a true D0→Dn cumulative LTV. It reflects how much revenue the day's new-user cohort generated on that specific day, not their total lifetime value.

results.question: LTV this month
results.answerText:
Daily cohort LTV ({{field:game_key_metrics.rev}} ÷ {{field:game_key_metrics.nru}}) for June 2026. The first 10 days show a wide range — Jun 5 spiked to ~398K VND/NRU (likely a revenue event or promotion), while the baseline hovers around 110–155K VND. The artifact opens the full month in Playground.

> ⚠️ **Metric note:** This is the **daily cohort LTV** — revenue on a given day divided by new users registered on that same day. It is **not** a true cumulative D0→Dn LTV (which would require summing revenue attributed to the same cohort across their first N days). Treat it as a same-day revenue efficiency proxy per NRU.

results.question: compare LTV month over month
results.answerText:
**LTV MoM: +28.8%** (May → June)

| | May 2026 | June 2026 MTD | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| {{field:recharge.arppu_vnd}} | 19,259 VND | 24,807 VND | **+28.8%** |
| {{field:recharge.paying_users_exact}} | 8,703 | 18,339 | +110% |
| {{field:recharge.revenue_vnd_real}} | 6.5B VND | 17.0B VND | +161%* |

*June revenue and payer count are not directly comparable — 22/30 days elapsed.

**Two caveats to keep in mind:**

1. **ARPPU ≠ LTV** — The certified `ltv` business metric is `rev ÷ NRU` (per new registered user). NRU isn't in the `recharge` cube, and the pre-aggregated `game_key_metrics` mart only has June data. ARPPU (÷ paying users) is the closest safe proxy across both months.

2. **May is the game's first recorded month** — the dataset starts 2026-05, so there's no prior history to trend further back.
results.artifactTitle: LTV Proxy (ARPPU) — May vs June 2026 MoM

> **Heads up:** {{field:active_daily.mau}} computes distinct users active in the rolling 30-day window anchored to each day. For a cleaner single-number snapshot of current trailing MAU, try {{field:active_daily.trailing_mau}} instead.

results.question: show Returning user last 7 days

Returning users ({{field:active_daily.dau}}) trended at **~179K on Jun 16** and **~175K on Jun 17**. Jun 18 shows only ~36K — likely an incomplete day due to pipeline lag of 3–4 days. No data for Jun 19–22 yet.

results.question: Returning user this month
results.answerText:
**495,501 unique users** active in June so far (trailing MAU, Jun 1–22).

The daily {{field:active_daily.dau}} line tells the fuller story: opened the month strong at ~191K on Jun 1, peaked around **196–197K on Jun 2–3**, then drifted down steadily to **~176K by Jun 17** — roughly a 10% slide over three weeks. Jun 18 shows only 36K which is a pipeline lag artefact, not a real drop; data through Jun 17 is reliable.

> **Note:** There's no dedicated "returning user" (re-engaged churner) measure in this game's schema. The metric above captures all active users. If you want churner re-engagement specifically, I can build a segment filter (e.g. users inactive for N days before appearing again this month) — just let me know the inactivity window.

The query uses {{field:user_active_monthly.mau}} filtered by {{field:user_active_monthly.cohort_type}} = `return`, grouped by {{field:user_active_monthly.log_year_month}}. You can swap in `new` or `old` to compare cohort composition side by side.

results.question: ROAS this month
results.answerText:
Daily ROAS for CFM VN in June 2026 is tracking well above break-even. From the first 10 days of the month:

- **Baseline** sits around **6–8×** on most days ({{field:game_key_metrics.roas}} = revenue ÷ ad spend)
- **Jun 5 spike to ~20×** — revenue hit ~1.75B VND against ~87M VND spend, likely a top-up event or campaign that punched well above normal
- **Jun 3 was the softest** at ~5.4×, with the lowest revenue day (~504M VND)

The artifact opens the full 30-day view in the Playground, with {{field:game_key_metrics.rev}} and {{field:game_key_metrics.cost_vnd}} alongside ROAS so you can drill into any spike or dip.

results.question: compare ROAS month over month
results.answerText:
Here's your ROAS MoM comparison artifact. It uses `compareDateRange` to overlay **June 1–22** against **May 1–22** — same 22-day window so spend timing isn't inflated by month-end.

**What the preview revealed for June:**
- Most days: {{field:game_key_metrics.roas}} sits in the **5×–11×** band
- Notable spike on **Jun 5: ~20×** (likely a low-spend / high-revenue day)

Open the card to see May overlaid — if June's line runs consistently above May's, ROAS is trending up MoM; if it crosses below, dig into cost increases or revenue softness by `media_source` or `country_code`.

**Next drill-downs worth running:**
- ROAS by `media_source` — which channel is driving the delta?
- ROAS by `platform` — iOS vs Android split
- Pair with {{field:game_key_metrics.cost_vnd}} + {{field:game_key_metrics.rev}} to see if the ratio shift is cost-driven or revenue-driven

results.question: compare New spender month over month
results.answerText:
Here's your MoM comparison for **New Spenders (NPU)** — users who made their **first-ever payment** in the period.

**What you're comparing:**
- 🟦 **June 2026** (Jun 1–22, MTD)
- 🟧 **May 2026** (May 1–22, same window — equal days for a fair like-for-like)

The two series are overlaid daily so you can spot day-by-day acceleration or deceleration. From the preview, June opened strong (140 NPU on Jun 1) and has been running ~86–140/day over the first 22 days.

**Want to extend or slice this?** A few natural next steps:
- Break down NPU by `media_source` or `country_code` to see which acquisition channels are driving the change
- Add {{field:game_key_metrics.nnpu}} (same-day new registrant + first payer) alongside NPU to track conversion tightness
- Switch the window to full May vs June-MTD if you prefer seeing May's complete month total

results.question: compare Revenue month over month
results.answerText:
Here's the MoM revenue comparison for cfm_vn — {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} plotted as two daily series.

**What the chart shows:**
- **Jun 2026 (to-date):** Jun 1–22 daily revenue — ranges ~504M–1.7B VND/day, with a notable spike on Jun 5 (~1.75B VND)
- **May 2026 (same window):** May 1–22, same day count for an apples-to-apples comparison

The Playground card overlays both periods on a shared date axis. Use the **indexed view** (rebases each series to 100) to compare momentum if one month's absolute values dwarf the other.

> ⚠️ Note: The deeplink normalised the date range — if the Playground shows only one period, manually set the second date range in the UI's time picker using "Compare" mode, or let me know and I'll rebuild as two separate queries.

results.question: compare ARPU month over month
results.answerText:
**Metric note:** The business-metric `mf_users.arpu_vnd` is a lifetime snapshot — it has no monthly time axis. The query uses {{field:user_recharge_monthly.arppu_vnd_monthly}} (revenue ÷ monthly paying users), which is the correct monthly-grain equivalent.

---

**May → June MoM snapshot**

| Metric | May 2026 | Jun 2026 *(partial)* | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| {{field:user_recharge_monthly.arppu_vnd_monthly}} | **1,166,255 VND** | 882,171 VND | −24% |
| {{field:user_recharge_monthly.revenue_vnd_total}} | 48.96B VND | 17.03B VND | −65% (22 days) |
| {{field:user_recharge_monthly.paying_users_monthly}} | 41,981 | 19,300 | −54% (22 days) |

**Key pattern in the 6-month trend:** paying user count has been contracting steadily since Jan 2026 (163K → 42K → 19K partial), while ARPPU climbed — the remaining base is a concentrated, high-spend cohort. May's spike to 1.17M is the local peak; June is tracking lower, but with only 22/~30 days elapsed the final figure may settle higher.

> ⚠️ June numbers are partial. Direct MoM % comparisons against May are premature until month-end.

results.question: compare DAU month over month
results.answerText:
Artifact is live. Click the card to open it in `/build` — the Playground renders both series as an overlaid multi-line chart (Jun in one color, May in another) so you can spot day-by-day divergence instantly.

**What the query covers**
- **Period A:** Jun 1–22, 2026 (current month to date)
- **Period B:** May 1–22, 2026 (same 22-day window — apples-to-apples)
- **Metric:** {{field:active_daily.dau}} (HLL approx-distinct active users per day)

From the preview, June is running **~185k–197k DAU** in the first 10 days. Open the full chart to see whether May tracked higher, lower, or flat across the same window — and use the Playground's **indexed view** (rebases both series to 100 on day 1) if the absolute scale makes the gap hard to read.
